name: Ned Land
description: Ned Land, the Canadian harpooner, would be rendered in a vibrant, slightly rough watercolor style. His face, sun-kissed and weathered like aged leather, is framed by a thick mane of dark brown hair, perpetually tousled by sea winds and rendered with loose, expressive strokes that bleed slightly into his tanned skin. His features are sharply defined: a broad, flat nose hinting at his rugged life, a strong, square jaw shadowed with stubble, and piercing, sky-blue eyes that sparkle with a mixture of intensity and good humor. These eyes are framed by crow's feet etched by years of squinting at the horizon, the details softened and blurred in the watercolor style. He wears a thick, fisherman’s smock in shades of deep indigo and teal, its edges faded and stained with seawater and rendered with short, staccato brushstrokes that suggest the rough texture of the fabric. Underneath, a glimpse of a crimson flannel shirt adds a splash of warmth. Around his waist, a wide, brown leather belt, subtly textured with painted cracks and scratches, holds a sheathed hunting knife with a bone handle, the hilt catching the light with a glossy, wet-on-wet effect. His hands are large and calloused, stained with dye that resembles dried blood, sketched with bold, confident strokes that capture their strength. The overall palette leans towards cool blues, greens, and browns, punctuated by the warm reds and yellows that highlight his skin and bring his character to life.
